selected_issue.issue: canonical tests fail after documented bootstrap because test dependencies are not installed
selected_issue.title: scripts/bootstrap.ps1 installs runtime deps only, causing python3 -m pytest -q failure in clean setup

why_it_outranked_alternatives: Current evidence showed no rank-1 structural violations and no rank-3/4 replay or diagnostic failures. A rank-2 failure was reproducible in this run: python3 -m pytest -q initially failed with 'No module named pytest' on agent-base, so this issue outranked lower-tier candidates.

checks.result: baseline showed bootstrap installing requirements.txt only

files_changed.change: Install requirements-dev.txt (with existence guard) instead of requirements.txt-only install.
Added human-readable promotion report for this run.
Added machine-readable promotion report for this run.

validation_performed.result: pass; bootstrap now points to requirements-dev.txt

before_after_evidence.before: Canonical pytest failed in clean run context (No module named pytest); bootstrap installed runtime requirements only.
before_after_evidence.after: Bootstrap now installs requirements-dev.txt, and canonical pytest executed successfully in this run (361 passed).

unresolved_risks: PowerShell bootstrap execution could not be run directly in this Linux sandbox because pwsh is unavailable.
Dev dependencies remain unpinned in requirements-dev.txt and may drift over time.
stop_reason: Selected rank-2 issue fixed with minimal bounded change and validated; further edits would exceed this single-issue run scope.
